A1.title: Which managed hosting provider does Acme want to use?

A2.title: Will Acme hold the hosting contract directly, or via EC?

A3.title: Are there any compliance constraints (SOC2, HIPAA, FedRAMP, data residency) that constrain the hosting choice?

B1.title: Can Acme provide a current database export for the migration?

B2.title: Who at Acme owns the migrate/drop editorial decisions, and what cadence works for review?

B3.title: Are there any content areas (gated portals, password-protected sections, internal-only PDFs) that won't show up in a public crawl?

C1.title: For the Salesforce sync — is it one-way (site → SF) or two-way?

C2.title: Should we build the newsletter integration against Mailchimp or HubSpot?

C3.title: Any other third-party systems the site needs to talk to (analytics beyond GA4, marketing automation, CRM, payment, search appliance)?

D1.title: Who manages the DNS cutover, and what's the rollback plan if something breaks at flip time?

D2.title: Is there a content freeze planned, and how will in-flight content from the old site be reconciled on launch day?

A1.title: When does IIE plan to sign the Pantheon contract?

A2.title: While the Pantheon contract is being finalized, can EC begin development on an EC-managed sandbox server, or would IIE prefer to stand up a Pantheon sandbox up-front?

B1.title: Final effort on obtaining a D7 database dump :)

B2.title: How will IIE provide editorial review on the migrate/drop decisions?

B3.title: Beyond the gated content we've already identified, are there other hidden pages, downloads, or asset libraries we should know about?

B4.title: Does IIE agree with the overall migration strategy and risks?

C1.title: What kinds of content do REACs, REAC Assistants, and Sponsors *create* (vs. only review)?

C2.title: When a user logs in via OKTA, what attributes will OKTA send to the new site?

C3.title: Is MFA (multi-factor authentication) enforced on your OKTA tenant?

C4.title: How will Sponsors (State Department staff) authenticate to the new site?

C5.title: How can you provide technical details on OKTA integration?

D1.title: Is there an authoritative list of accredited U.S. higher-education institutions we should validate submissions against?

D2.title: When a non-accredited person submits something, what should the system do — reject silently, reject with a message, route to manual review, or something else?

E1.title: The number and type of maps in the site will depend on the data that needs to be shown in each use case

E2.title: The current site has two parallel country lists — should the new build consolidate?

E3.title: The RFP names several IIE-owned and external resources HEIs should access from the new site — what's the format, refresh cadence, and intended role for each?

E4.title: The map's data layers — which do you want, and what's the source for each?

E5.title: Anything else about the map we should be considering?

F1.title: Scholarship filter vocabularies — confirm existing and fill gaps.

F3.title: Adviser event sub-categorization — what event types within "adviser events" do you want to differentiate?

F5.title: Does IIE agree with the overall approach to Scholarships and Events?

G1.title: Do you have a preferred LLM provider for the AI features?

G2.title: The LLM and Vector DB contracts will be held by IIE directly — can you please confirm?

G3.title: What's your vision for the Greeter / chat assistant?

G4.title: What's your vision for AI-enhanced search?

G5.title: What's your vision for AI-assisted editorial tagging?

G6.title: Does IIE agree with the overall AI feature approach?

H1.title: Which content types should NOT appear in public search results?

I1.title: MailChimp setup for the new site's newsletter signup.

I2.title: Analytics tooling — a few questions to align on.

I3.title: Will the new site require a cookie-consent banner (e.g., for EU visitors under GDPR or California users under CCPA)?

J1.title: Permanent 301 redirects for legacy `educationusa.state.gov` URLs + the State Dept POC who can implement them.

J3.title: SSL certificate — Pantheon-managed Let's Encrypt, or do you need a custom certificate?

J5.title: Outbound email from the site — what SMTP service will we use?

J6.title: Sender domain for outbound email from the site.

K1.title: Does IIE have a GitHub or GitLab organization you'd like the project repository to live in, or should we host it in Electric Citizen's GitHub organization with IIE granted access?

L1.title: Scholarship records — migration scope and View-preservation.

L2.title: Video — how should the new build present the existing video collection?

L3.title: Campus news stories — does this exist as a public-facing content stream on the current site, or only inside the logged-in portal? If public, what URL pattern?

L4.title: Webinars — `/us-higher-education-professionals/online-services/webinars` is linked from the Online Services nav but 301-redirects to the homepage.

L5.title: EducationUSA Academy content — the program ended summer 2024, but its content remains on the live site.

L6.title: Adviser profiles — the LOA references "adviser profiles" as content the new site should support, but the current public-facing site does not surface any.

L9.title: Country Fact Sheets — confirm scope and visibility.

L10.title: Spam protection — the current site uses Drupal image CAPTCHA on one form (`/node/1116`) and nothing on anything else.

L12.title: Region landing pages — what should the new build do with the 12 `/regions/{slug}` pages?

L13.title: Student Success Stories — do you want them on the new site?

M1.title: Campus Hosting submission workflow — currently four external Google Forms power four distinct application paths

M2.title: Event registration is uniformly routed off-site

M3.title: Any other third-party form tools on the current site we haven't surfaced?

A1.title: What sign-on system do staff already use for other applications? Do you plan to use this same system for Drupal authentication?
A1.sectionTitle: Authentication, Single Sign-On, and User Roles

A2.title: Can your current SSO system pass any details at login (e.g. office/district and role)?
A2.sectionTitle: Authentication, Single Sign-On, and User Roles

A3.title: How do editors work today, and what editor types and roles do you need going forward?
A3.sectionTitle: Authentication, Single Sign-On, and User Roles

A4.title: Is multi-factor sign-in required?
A4.sectionTitle: Authentication, Single Sign-On, and User Roles

B1.title: For the editor role, what review process do you want before content goes live (e.g. self-publish, or a review and approval step)?

B2.title: Should editors be able to edit anywhere, or be limited to their district or office area?

B3.title: Who owns content across the districts and central offices? Is there a central web team, or is it fully distributed across districts and offices?

C1.title: Starting from the base list above, which types fit MnDOT as-is, and what additional types are you considering?

C2.title: Construction projects are built today as standalone microsites (e.g. `/35w94/`, `/i94-mg-clearwater/`). We assume that these become a structured Project content type as a key feature of the new site?

C3.title: News already tags every release by district (in the central `/news/` feed), with older per-district archives alongside. Should news be one content type with a district field, and do districts still publish their own releases, or is it centralized now?

C4.title: Reference data like District, Highway, and City appears inconsistently across the current site. We assume these should be managed as standardized taxonomy lists? Are there any other known metadata/taxonomies that you can identify?

D1.title: Does the construction data API already exist, or will MnDOT build it? At a high level, what will it provide (e.g. project status, location, schedule) and how often does it refresh?

D2.title: How should this data show up on the site: a list of project pages, a construction map, or both?

D3.title: For the construction map, should it be built and owned in your ArcGIS Online environment (with the new site feeding it project data), or built natively in the new site?

D4.title: At a high level, are the specific layers and basemaps a construction map would need already shared publicly (or easily made public) in your ArcGIS Online org, or are they internal, on an Enterprise portal, or licensed in a way that limits sharing?

E1.title: We recommend building search on Drupal's Search API with a Solr backend (a dedicated search engine suited to large sites and fast filtering). Will your environment support Solr or a hosted search service, or should we plan around Drupal's built-in database search?

E2.title: Alongside global site search, we'd build a dedicated construction-project finder (filterable by district, highway, status). Does that match how you want projects surfaced?

E3.title: Is there any need to keep or maintain the existing `mn.gov` state search or the Google project search after launch?

E4.title: Beyond the construction-project finder, are there other content or data sets you'd want searchable or browsable in their own right, for example office/staff directories, rest areas, locations, documents and PDFs, or an existing database you'd want indexed?

E5.title: AI-enhanced search was not mentioned in the current scope, but it's an option to consider and might be the strongest lever for increasing self-service levels and addressing issues found in your site feedback data. Would you want us to outline any of these options?

F1.title: We recommend rebuilding intake forms natively in Drupal (Webform module). Does that fit, and are there any forms you'd prefer to keep on an external tool?

F2.title: For the report form, where do submissions go today and how are they tracked? (e.g. email, a queue, a case or CRM system?) And how should that work on the new site?

F3.title: The current feedback form appears prominently on every page. Is this still desired in the new build?

F4.title: Are there any other forms (ombudsman, ADA, commercial-vehicle, tort claims, bike map requests, property sales) that might benefit from any type of integration with other systems?

G1.title: Are the above assumptions correct? Are there other systems, internal applications, or integrations your site connects to that wouldn't be visible from the outside?

H1.title: How far back do you plan to go with the news migration? The site holds years of news (the current feed plus legacy district archives).

H2.title: For news, do you want us to recommend an assisted import rather than hand-entering everything? Is there other high-volume content to consider such as events?

H3.title: What's the plan for PDFs and other documents? The site links to many (plans, maps, reports), and legacy document accessibility is out of scope per the Q&A.

I1.title: On the new site, do you want tags managed through Google Tag Manager rather than hardcoded as they are today?

I2.title: We assume you will continue using Siteimprove. Are you using the new Siteimprove.ai Intelligence Platform, or any of their accessibility checkers?

J1.title: GovDelivery (email bulletins and subscriptions): how should the new site work with it, and do you need separate subscription lists (for example, by district or topic) with sign-up embeds on the site?

J2.title: Transactional email (form confirmations, password resets, system notices): what should the new site use to send it?

J3.title: We assume URL redirects will be handled within Drupal, with your team creating the redirect import file. Does that work?

J4.title: We understand the standard is WCAG 2.2 AA (layered on the Minnesota Digital Accessibility Standard: Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA), that accessibility is built into our design deliverables and theme prototype, that automated and manual audits happen before launch, and that legacy PDF accessibility is out of scope. Are there other accessibility concerns or considerations we should account for?

J5.title: Multilingual support isn't mentioned in the RFP or Q&A, so we're assuming the new site is English-only with no content translation. Is that correct?
